problem: What material stores hydrogen fuel at high density without safety risk?
mechanism: Biomimetic surface structure (lotus, shark skin, gecko)
hypothesis: What material stores hydrogen fuel at high density without safety risk? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Biomimetic. Biomimetic surface structure (lotus, shark skin, gecko) directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mechanical testing (tensile, fatigue) with 21 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Real knowledge is to know the extent of one's ignorance. — Confucius
